CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-14-2022 Set For Trial On Jun-19-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Andrew Contopoulos, Esq., Law Offices of Andrew N. Contopoulos, 510-910-3258, for Plaintiff Barbara Pitts.
Susan Nikdel, Esq., TROUTMAN PEPPER HAMILTON SANDERS LLP, 415.477.5700 susan.nikdel@troutman.com, for Defendant via Zoom Conference.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, NA's demurrer is overruled.

The complaint alleges, in sum, that a bag of gold coins disappeared from plaintiff's safety deposit box after defendant's employee improperly watched her put the bag in the box and made copies of the key for it. (Id. at 4:24-5:26.)

"Economic Loss Doctrine." This concept does not apply here because the pled wrongful acts "may constitute both a breach of contract and an invasion of an interest protected by the law of torts." (North American Chemical Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 764, 774.)

Conversion. Whether the bank's employees substantially deviated from their job duties is a factual issue that remains to be developed.

Failure of Bailment. This cause of action is adequately pled. (See Gebert v. Yank (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 544, 551.) As noted above, California courts liberally allow pleading counts in the alternative.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
